mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.0 percent, it signals a confirmed labor market recovery that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion. This joint signal reduces inventory-to-sales ratios and accelerates earnings revisions, producing positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST change × INDPRO change) yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day SPX returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market return in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During an early tightening cycle, rising policy rates combined with persistent inflation compress equity risk premiums and trigger sector rotation away from growth stocks, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of early tightening episodes, or the combined rate-inflation signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting short-term equity declines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding a 2.5 percent annualized rate sustains corporate profit margins and validates earnings growth expectations, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day investment horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent signals supply chain disruption or sudden demand contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in over 60% of instances following a >1.5 percent INDPRO drop, or the signal fails to predict returns after adjusting for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 200,000 and unemployment falling below 5.5 percent creates a tight labor market that accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive short-term equity returns as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 5-day SPX returns, or walk-forward backtesting shows a win rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, rising retail sales and consumer credit indicate pent-up demand being released into the economy, which drives cyclical equity outperformance and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of post-recession recovery periods despite rising RSAFS, or the signal shows no predictive power when controlling for GDP growth trends.
